summary: Completed frontend-only UI/UX testing of CivicTrack Portal across all 9 pages: Landing, Feed, Submit wizard, Issue Detail, Map, Dashboard, Profile, Admin, Official. Validated navigation, language switcher (EN/HI/TE), header CTAs, filters, sorting, comment posting, chart rendering, moderation actions, and footer. One CRITICAL bug found in Submit wizard at Step 4 (Auto-detect Location) that blocks the entire submission flow when user relies on GPS.

ui_bugs.issue: Clicking 'Auto-detect location' only fills the State field. Address, Pincode, and City remain empty because the update() helper uses stale-closure form state (setForm({...form, [k]: v})). When called 4 times in the same click handler, only the LAST setState wins. Because canProceed() requires address, city AND state, the Continue button stays DISABLED, completely blocking the submission flow via GPS. User has to type Address & City manually, defeating the purpose of GPS auto-detect.

action_items: Fix Submit.jsx update() function to use the functional updater form: `const update = (k, v) => setForm(prev => ({ ...prev, [k]: v }))`. Alternatively, refactor the GPS button to do a single setForm({...form, address:..., pincode:..., city:..., state:...}). After fix, GPS button should populate all 4 fields and Continue button should enable immediately.
critical_code_review_comments: Submit.jsx line 38: `const update = (k, v) => setForm({ ...form, [k]: v })` is the stale-closure anti-pattern; any sync sequence of update() calls in one handler drops all but the last key. Same pattern could silently break other flows if more multi-field updates are added.
Submit.jsx GPS handler (line 223-230) performs 4 sequential update() calls — this is the exact scenario that surfaces the stale closure bug.

i18n.js only translates nav + hero + common + status keys; page bodies (Feed header, Submit step labels, IssueCard labels, etc.) remain English in HI/TE. Language switcher is effectively cosmetic for nav/hero only. Consider expanding translations or documenting this limitation.

success_rate.frontend: 94% — 15/16 feature areas pass; Submit GPS flow blocked

seed_data_creation: None — all data is static mock from /app/frontend/src/lib/mockData.js
context_for_next_testing_agent: All data-testids are in place. Nav, language switcher (EN/HI/TE), Feed search+filter+sort, Issue Detail comment posting, Map markers+mode toggle, Dashboard Recharts (8 charts rendered), Profile tabs, Admin moderation (4 approve/4 reject), Official queue (12 items) with update/proof/escalate toasts — all verified working. 0 console errors, 0 page errors. Only remaining issue: Submit.jsx stale-closure bug in update() preventing GPS auto-detect from filling all 4 location fields. After the fix, retest just the GPS button click → verify address, pincode, city, state all populated → Continue enables → Step 5 Review shows full location → submit-final-btn triggers success toast and redirects to /feed.
rca of the issue: ROOT CAUSE: Submit.jsx line 38 defines `const update = (k, v) => setForm({ ...form, [k]: v })`. The inline arrow captures `form` by closure at render time. When the GPS button's onClick handler calls update() 4 times in one synchronous tick (lines 225-228), all 4 invocations read the SAME stale `form` reference (the one captured during the current render), compute `{...form, address:...}`, `{...form, pincode:...}`, `{...form, city:...}`, `{...form, state:...}` — all using the ORIGINAL form (no address/pincode/city in it) — and React batches them, so the last setForm call wins. Result: only state='Karnataka' persists; address/pincode/city stay empty. REPRODUCTION: /submit → pick any category → fill title(>=10) + description(>=20) → Continue → Continue through Media → on Step 4 click 'Auto-detect location' → observe only State is filled; Continue stays disabled. MITIGATION: Change to functional form: `const update = (k, v) => setForm(prev => ({ ...prev, [k]: v }))`. This makes each setter read fresh prev state. Alternative: batch into a single setForm call inside the GPS handler.
